summary: Decent audience tape captures a strong second set with a standout Sugaree and fluid improvisations.
review: The show is praised for its many highlights, especially an excellent second set and the superb blending of three big breaks that culminate in a powerful crescendo. Recording quality is described as a decent 1976 audience capture – clear enough to hear crowd banter but with some hollow low‑end character. Overall the performance shines despite the modest tape fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently note a good musical performance (highlights, strong second set, Sugaree) while describing the recording as merely decent audience tape, leading to an overall rating just above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Decent 1976 audience recording – captures crowd banter and overall mix but has a hollow spot in the lows and lacks the clarity of a soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows well with three notable improvisational breaks, a strong crescendo in the third segment, and an especially good second set that many reviewers cite as a highlight.
